title: Human Pancreas Optical Clearing by iDISCO and VISIKOL
author: Martha Campbell-Thompson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ba4qigvw

steps.instruction: On a shaker, wash the fixed tissue 2x30 minutes in 1X PBS at room temperature.
Dehydrate the tissue in increasing grades of methanol, 50%-80%-100% MeOH, for 1 hour each at room temperature.
Place the tissue in 5% H 2 O 2 /20% DMSO/MeOH overnight on a shaker at room temperature.
Wash tissue 2x1 hour in 20% DMSO/MeOH at room temperature.
Wash tissue in decreasing grades of methanol, 100%- 80%-50% MeOH, for 1 hour each at room temperature.
Place tissue in 1X PBS with 1% TritonX-100 for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Place the samples in Penetration Buffer for 36 hours at room temperature.
Transfer the samples to Blocking Buffer and incubate for 36 hours at room temperature.

Using the Antibody solution as a diluent, prepare the primary antibody cocktail and incubate the samples for 48 hours at room temperature. Primary and secondary antibodies are listed in the JOVE publication. We utilized cocktails of primary and secondary antibodies, combining primaries raised in mouse, rabbit, goat, and chicken and suitable secondary antibodies raised in donkey (ThermoFisher, Jackson Immunoresearch).
Wash the samples in the Wash Buffer 5x30 minutes at room temperature.
Using the Antibody solution as a diluent, prepare the secondary antibody cocktail and incubate the samples for 48 hours at room temperature.
Wash the samples in the Wash Buffer 5x30 minutes at room temperature.
Wash the tissue in increasing grades of methanol, 50%-80%-100% MeOH, for 1 hour each at room temperature.
Incubate the samples in HISTO-1 solution for 16 hours at room temperature.
Incubate the samples in HISTO-2 solution for 16 hours at room temperature.
Mount samples on glass slides in HISTO-2 with a #1.5 coverslip using silicon spacers or use glass chamber slides or small imaging dish.
Image using a confocal microscope or suitable Lightsheet.
